metadata.title: TELANGANA STATE AGRICULTURAL DATABASE (2026)
metadata.subtitle: Comprehensive Collection of Crops, Land Types, Diseases, and Organic Remedies

soilTypes.name: Red Sandy Loams (Chalka Soils)
soilTypes.teluguName: ఎర్ర ఇసుక బంకమట్టి (Erra Isuka Banka Matti)

soilTypes.name: Black Cotton Soils
soilTypes.characteristics: High clay content, swells when wet, cracks when dry, fertile

soilTypes.name: Shallow to Medium Black Soils
soilTypes.teluguName: లోతు లేని నల్ల మట్టి (Lothu Leni Nalla Matti)
soilTypes.characteristics: Less deep than very deep black soils, moderate fertility

soilTypes.name: Lateritic Soils
soilTypes.characteristics: Acidic, iron-rich, poor fertility, found in plateau regions

soilTypes.name: Alluvial Soils
soilTypes.characteristics: Fertile, high nutrients, suitable for intensive cropping

soilTypes.name: Salt-Affected Soils
soilTypes.characteristics: High salinity/alkalinity, poor productivity, requires soil amendment

crops.name: Paddy (Rice)
crops.suitableSoilTypes: Alluvial soils, black soils (irrigated), red soils (with irrigation)
crops.majorDistricts: Warangal, Karimnagar, Khammam, Nalgonda, Nizamabad, Medak, Adilabad

crops.name: Maize (Corn)
crops.suitableSoilTypes: Red sandy loams, black soils, alluvial soils
crops.majorDistricts: Adilabad, Nirmal, Nizamabad, Karimnagar, Warangal, Khammam
crops.importance: Feed crop, food grain, poultry industry

crops.name: Red Gram (Pigeon Pea, Tur/Arhar)
crops.majorDistricts: Mahabubnagar, Nagarkurnool, Wanaparthy, Medak, Adilabad, Khammam

crops.name: Green Gram (Moong)
crops.suitableSoilTypes: Red sandy loams, black soils, alluvial soils
crops.importance: Short duration, high protein, summer crop

crops.name: Cotton (Fiber crop but counted in commercial crops)
crops.majorDistricts: Adilabad, Nirmal, Nizamabad, Warangal, Karimnagar, Nalgonda, Mahabubnagar
crops.importance: Cash crop, textile industry, cottonseed oil
crops.popularVarieties: Bt Cotton (RCH 2, NCS 207, Jadoo), Desi cotton (MCU 5)

crops.name: Groundnut (Peanut)
crops.suitableSoilTypes: Red sandy loams, red loamy sands

crops.name: Sunflower
crops.status: VERIFIED - Major oilseed (Wikipedia mentions recent favor)
crops.suitableSoilTypes: Black soils, red soils, alluvial soils

crops.name: Soybean
crops.importance: Protein crop, oil production, industrial use

crops.name: Chilli (Red Pepper)
crops.suitableSoilTypes: Red sandy loams, black soils, alluvial soils
crops.majorDistricts: Warangal, Khammam, Mahabubnagar, Karimnagar, Guntur (border areas)
crops.importance: Export commodity, spice industry, high value

crops.name: Turmeric
crops.importance: Spice, export, medicinal use, high value

crops.name: Ajwain (Carom Seeds)
crops.status: VERIFIED - 30.5% national share (Wikipedia) - Highest in India!
crops.importance: Medicinal, spice, export, very high value

crops.name: Brinjal (Eggplant)
crops.suitableSoilTypes: Red sandy loams, black soils, alluvial soils
crops.popularVarieties: Pusa Purple Long, Arka Anand, Anjali

crops.name: Bitter Gourd
crops.popularVarieties: Pusa Do Mausami, CO 1, Arka Harit

crops.name: Bottle Gourd
crops.popularVarieties: Pusa Summer Prolific Long, Arka Bahar

crops.name: Watermelon
crops.popularVarieties: Sugar Baby, Arka Manik, Crimson Sweet

crops.name: Gherkin (Pickle Cucumber)
crops.status: VERIFIED - Export crop (Wikipedia mentions export zones)
crops.majorDistricts: Mahabubnagar, Rangareddy, Medak, Karimnagar, Warangal (contract farming)

crops.name: Lemon
crops.suitableSoilTypes: Red sandy loams, all well-drained soils

crops.name: Banana
crops.popularVarieties: Robusta (Poovan), Nendran, Grand Naine, Elakki bale

crops.name: Papaya
crops.popularVarieties: Red Lady, Taiwan Red, Pusa Dwarf

crops.name: Coconut
crops.majorDistricts: Limited to irrigated areas - Khammam, Nalgonda, Warangal (gardens)
crops.popularVarieties: West Coast Tall, Kalpa Sankara hybrid

crops.name: Tobacco
crops.types: Virginia tobacco, Burley tobacco, Natu tobacco

crops.name: Oil Palm
crops.importance: Edible oil, government promotion under oil palm mission

crops.name: Grapes
crops.status: VERIFIED - Export crop (Wikipedia mentions export zones)
crops.suitableSoilTypes: Red sandy loams, well-drained black soils

crops.name: Bean
crops.popularVarieties: Arka Komal (French beans), Pusa Navbahar (cluster beans)

crops.name: Potato
crops.status: VERIFIED - Growing crop for urban market
crops.suitableSoilTypes: Red sandy loams, alluvial soils (well-drained), black soils (with good drainage)
crops.importance: Food security crop, urban consumption, high nutritional value
crops.popularVarieties: Kufri Jyoti, Kufri Pukhraj, Kufri Chandramukhi

crops.name: Ginger
crops.suitableSoilTypes: Red sandy loams, alluvial soils (well-drained, high organic matter)
crops.importance: Spice crop, medicinal value, export commodity, high returns

crops.name: Garlic
crops.suitableSoilTypes: Well-drained red sandy loams, alluvial soils, light black soils
crops.importance: Condiment, medicinal properties, local consumption, export potential
crops.popularVarieties: Agrifound White (G-41), Bhima Omkar, Yamuna Safed

crops.name: Spinach
crops.suitableSoilTypes: Alluvial soils, black soils, red sandy loams (fertile, moisture-retentive)
crops.importance: Leafy vegetable, high iron and vitamin content, daily consumption
crops.popularVarieties: All Green, Pusa All Green, local varieties

crops.name: Broccoli
crops.suitableSoilTypes: Well-drained loamy soils, red sandy loams, alluvial soils (pH 6.0-7.0)
crops.importance: Premium vegetable, urban/export market, high nutritional value, cancer-fighting compounds
crops.popularVarieties: Palam Samridhi, Green Head, Aishwarya, KTS-1

crops.name: Ivy Gourd
crops.suitableSoilTypes: All soil types (adaptable), thrives in red soils and alluvial soils
crops.season: Year-round (perennial vine), peak production summer and monsoon
crops.importance: Common vegetable, anti-diabetic properties, backyard cultivation

crops.name: Broad Beans
crops.teluguName: చిక్కుడు కాయ (Chikkudu Kaya) / అవరే కాయి (Avare Kayi)
crops.suitableSoilTypes: Well-drained loamy soils, alluvial soils, black soils
crops.importance: Protein-rich vegetable, winter season crop, nutritious green vegetable
crops.popularVarieties: Early Prolific, Windsor Longpod, local varieties

crops.name: Cluster Beans (Guar)
crops.suitableSoilTypes: Red sandy loams, light soils, drought-tolerant (grows in poor soils)
crops.importance: Vegetable, guar gum industrial use, drought-resistant, soil improver

crops.name: Mint
crops.suitableSoilTypes: Moist fertile soils, alluvial soils, well-drained black soils
crops.season: Year-round (perennial), best growth in cool season
crops.majorDistricts: All districts (kitchen gardens), commercial in Rangareddy, Medak
crops.importance: Culinary herb, medicinal (digestive), essential oil extraction, chutney ingredient

crops.name: Gongura (Red Sorrel)
crops.status: VERIFIED - Cultural significance, Telangana's signature leafy vegetable
crops.suitableSoilTypes: All soil types, thrives in red soils and alluvial soils
crops.importance: Telangana's iconic sour leafy vegetable, rich in vitamin C, antioxidants, pickle ingredient
crops.popularVarieties: Red variety (Erra Gongura), Green variety (Chinta Gongura)

PADDY (RICE).name: Rice Blast (Leaf, Neck, Nodal, Collar)
symptoms: White to gray-green lesions with darker borders on leaves
PADDY (RICE).favorableConditions: High humidity (>90%), 25-28°C, long leaf wetness periods, excessive nitrogen
PADDY (RICE).occurrenceInTelangana: Very common in Warangal, Karimnagar, Khammam, Nalgonda (rainy season)
PADDY (RICE).organicRemedies: Trichoderma viride, Pseudomonas fluorescens, Neem oil, Panchagavya

PADDY (RICE).name: Bacterial Blight
PADDY (RICE).teluguName: బ్యాక్టీరియా ఆకుముడత వ్యాధి (Bacteria Aku Mudatha Vyadhi)
symptoms: Yellowing and eventual drying (kresek stage in seedlings)
Milky bacterial ooze from cut stems
PADDY (RICE).favorableConditions: High temperature (25-34°C), high humidity, wounds, heavy rains
PADDY (RICE).occurrenceInTelangana: Common in all paddy-growing areas, Kharif season
PADDY (RICE).organicRemedies: Pseudomonas fluorescens seed treatment, Copper oxychloride (organic), Bordeaux mixture

PADDY (RICE).name: Brown Spot
symptoms: Circular to oval brown spots with gray/white centers
Affects leaves, leaf sheaths, panicles, grains (pecky rice)
PADDY (RICE).favorableConditions: Nutrient deficiency (especially K), drought stress, high humidity
PADDY (RICE).occurrenceInTelangana: Very common - deficiency-prone soils, upland red soils
PADDY (RICE).organicRemedies: Trichoderma viride, balanced nutrition (potash enhancement), Jeevamrutham

PADDY (RICE).name: Sheath Blight
symptoms: Oblong or ellipsoid greenish-gray lesions on leaf sheaths near water line
Can affect panicles in severe cases
PADDY (RICE).favorableConditions: High humidity, dense planting, excessive nitrogen, temperatures 28-32°C
PADDY (RICE).occurrenceInTelangana: Common in irrigated lowland areas - Warangal, Khammam, Nalgonda
PADDY (RICE).organicRemedies: Trichoderma harzianum, Pseudomonas fluorescens, plant spacing management

PADDY (RICE).name: False Smut
symptoms: Individual grains transformed into velvety green/greenish-black balls
Spore balls larger than normal grains
PADDY (RICE).favorableConditions: High humidity at flowering stage, temperatures 25-28°C, excessive nitrogen
PADDY (RICE).occurrenceInTelangana: Increasing incidence in high-input irrigated areas
PADDY (RICE).soilTypesAffected: Irrigated areas - alluvial and black soils
PADDY (RICE).organicRemedies: Pseudomonas fluorescens, copper-based fungicides, balanced fertilization

PADDY (RICE).name: Bacterial Leaf Streak
symptoms: Narrow, dark green to brown interveinal streaks
Streaks merge to form necrotic areas
Different from bacterial blight (starts interveinal, not from edges)
PADDY (RICE).favorableConditions: High temperature, high humidity, windy conditions
PADDY (RICE).occurrenceInTelangana: Sporadic occurrence, mainly in Kharif season
PADDY (RICE).organicRemedies: Copper spray, Pseudomonas fluorescens, resistant varieties

PADDY (RICE).name: Sheath Rot
symptoms: Oblong or irregular grayish-brown lesions on leaf sheaths
Boots (young panicles) may rot within sheath
Panicles may emerge partially or not at all
PADDY (RICE).favorableConditions: High humidity, heavy nitrogen, dense planting
PADDY (RICE).organicRemedies: Trichoderma viride, Pseudomonas, avoid excessive nitrogen

PADDY (RICE).name: Root Rot (Seedling Stage)
PADDY (RICE).status: NON-VERIFIED (Field observations, no formal diagnostic data)

PADDY (RICE).name: Grain Discoloration (Dirty Panicle)
symptoms: Dark brown to blackish discoloration of grains
Reduces grain quality and market value
May be associated with Pecky rice
PADDY (RICE).favorableConditions: High humidity at grain filling, insect damage
PADDY (RICE).occurrenceInTelangana: Common in late-harvested crops, rainy season
PADDY (RICE).organicRemedies: Timely harvest, Neem oil spray, proper field sanitation

COTTON.name: Cotton Wilt (Fusarium Wilt)
COTTON.status: VERIFIED (Major disease in India, ICAR documented)
symptoms: Sudden wilting of plants at any growth stage
Vascular browning (brown streaks in stem when cut)
COTTON.occurrenceInTelangana: Very common - all cotton-growing districts (Adilabad, Warangal, Karimnagar, Nalgonda)
COTTON.organicRemedies: Trichoderma viride seed/soil treatment, Pseudomonas fluorescens, crop rotation

COTTON.name: Bacterial Blight of Cotton
COTTON.occurrenceInTelangana: Common in Kharif season, rainy periods
COTTON.organicRemedies: Copper-based sprays, Pseudomonas fluorescens, resistant varieties

COTTON.name: Grey Mildew (Ashy Mildew)
symptoms: White powdery growth on lower leaf surface
COTTON.favorableConditions: High humidity, moderate temperature (20-28°C), cloudy weather
COTTON.organicRemedies: Neem oil + Panchagavya spray, sulfur dust

COTTON.name: Alternaria Leaf Spot
symptoms: Circular to irregular brown spots with concentric rings

COTTON.name: Root Rot (Multiple Pathogens)
COTTON.status: NON-VERIFIED (Field problem, mixed pathogen not clearly diagnosed)
COTTON.occurrenceInTelangana: Farmer reports from heavy soil areas
COTTON.organicRemedies: Trichoderma, drainage improvement, avoid excess irrigation

CHILLI.name: Chilli Leaf Curl Virus
CHILLI.teluguName: మిరపకాయ ఆకు ముడత వైరస్ (Mirapakaya Aku Mudatha Virus)
CHILLI.severity: VERY HIGH - Can cause total crop failure
CHILLI.organicRemedies: Neem oil for vector control, removal of infected plants, border crops (marigold), reflective mulch

CHILLI.name: Anthracnose (Fruit Rot)
symptoms: Pink/orange spore masses in humid conditions
CHILLI.organicRemedies: Trichoderma viride spray, Garlic bulb extract, proper drying

CHILLI.name: Damping Off (Seedling Disease)
symptoms: Post-emergence: Water-soaked stems at soil line, toppling of seedlings
Pre-emergence: Seeds/young seedlings rot before emergence
CHILLI.favorableConditions: Excess moisture, high seed density, poor drainage
CHILLI.occurrenceInTelangana: Common in nurseries across all districts
CHILLI.organicRemedies: Trichoderma seed treatment, proper drainage, soil solarization

CHILLI.name: Powdery Mildew
symptoms: White powdery growth on leaves (mostly lower surface)
CHILLI.favorableConditions: Moderate temperature (20-30°C), high humidity (but dry leaf surface)
CHILLI.organicRemedies: Sulfur dust, Neem oil + Panchagavya, cow urine spray

CHILLI.name: Bacterial Wilt
CHILLI.favorableConditions: High temperature (>30°C), high soil moisture, acidic soils
CHILLI.organicRemedies: Pseudomonas fluorescens application, lime treatment (pH correction), crop rotation

CHILLI.name: Mosaic Virus Complex
CHILLI.status: NON-VERIFIED (Farmers report leaf mottling, suspected virus but not lab-confirmed)

MAIZE.name: Turcicum Leaf Blight (Northern Corn Leaf Blight)
symptoms: Long, elliptical, grayish-green to tan lesions on leaves
MAIZE.occurrenceInTelangana: Common in Adilabad, Nirmal, Karimnagar (Kharif season)
MAIZE.organicRemedies: Trichoderma spray, resistant hybrids, crop residue removal

MAIZE.name: Maydis Leaf Blight (Southern Corn Leaf Blight)
symptoms: Small, tan, rectangular lesions parallel to leaf veins

MAIZE.name: Common Rust
symptoms: Circular to elongate, golden-brown pustules on both leaf surfaces
MAIZE.organicRemedies: Sulfur dust, resistant hybrids, early planting

MAIZE.name: Downy Mildew
symptoms: White downy growth on lower leaf surface
MAIZE.favorableConditions: Cool temperature (20-25°C), high humidity, morning dew
MAIZE.organicRemedies: Metalaxyl seed treatment (organic approved), resistant hybrids, field drainage

MAIZE.name: Stalk Rot Complex
MAIZE.status: NON-VERIFIED (Farmers report lodging, complex pathogen situation)
MAIZE.favorableConditions: High density, drought stress followed by irrigation
MAIZE.organicRemedies: Balanced fertilization, proper spacing, avoid late irrigation

SORGHUM (JOWAR).name: Grain Mold
symptoms: Black, gray, pink, or white mold on grains
SORGHUM (JOWAR).favorableConditions: High humidity during grain maturity, rainfall at harvest
SORGHUM (JOWAR).occurrenceInTelangana: Very common in Kharif sorghum (monsoon season)
SORGHUM (JOWAR).severity: HIGH - Major quality loss, mycotoxin contamination
SORGHUM (JOWAR).organicRemedies: Resistant varieties, timely harvest, proper drying

SORGHUM (JOWAR).name: Anthracnose
symptoms: Circular to elliptical tan lesions with purple borders on leaves
Midrib infection causing "top rot" or dead heart

SORGHUM (JOWAR).name: Downy Mildew
symptoms: White downy growth on lower leaf surface
Stunting, leaf shredding, no grain formation
SORGHUM (JOWAR).severity: HIGH (complete crop loss if infected early)
SORGHUM (JOWAR).organicRemedies: Metalaxyl seed treatment, resistant varieties, rogue infected plants

SORGHUM (JOWAR).name: Ergot (Sugary Disease)
symptoms: Replacement of grains with elongated dark brown/black sclerotia
SORGHUM (JOWAR).favorableConditions: Cool  temperatures (12-20°C), high humidity during flowering
SORGHUM (JOWAR).organicRemedies: Avoid Rabi cultivation in ergot-prone areas, resistant varieties

SORGHUM (JOWAR).name: Leaf Blight (Zonate Leaf Spot)
symptoms: Circular to oval spots with concentric zonation
Tan to reddish-brown lesions on leaves
SORGHUM (JOWAR).organicRemedies: Trichoderma spray, resistant varieties, field sanitation

SORGHUM (JOWAR).name: Rust
symptoms: Small circular to elongate purplish-red pustules on leaves
SORGHUM (JOWAR).occurrenceInTelangana: Common in late Kharif, Rabi seasons
SORGHUM (JOWAR).organicRemedies: Sulfur dust, resistant varieties, avoid excessive nitrogen

SORGHUM (JOWAR).name: Charcoal Rot
symptoms: Numerous tiny black sclerotia in stalk
SORGHUM (JOWAR).favorableConditions: High temperature + drought stress, poor soil
SORGHUM (JOWAR).occurrenceInTelangana: Common in drought years, Red sandy loams
SORGHUM (JOWAR).organicRemedies: Adequate irrigation, balanced fertilization, resistant varieties

PEARL MILLET (BAJRA).name: Downy Mildew
PEARL MILLET (BAJRA).status: VERIFIED (Wikipedia - List of Pearl Millet Diseases)
symptoms: White downy growth on lower leaf surface
"Green ear" - malformed panicle with leafy structures
PEARL MILLET (BAJRA).favorableConditions: Cool temperature (20-25°C), high humidity, morning dew
PEARL MILLET (BAJRA).severity: VERY HIGH - Can cause 30-40% yield loss
PEARL MILLET (BAJRA).organicRemedies: Metalaxyl seed treatment, resistant hybrids, timely sowing, rogue infected plants

PEARL MILLET (BAJRA).name: Ergot
symptoms: Replacement of grains with hard dark brown/purple sclerotia
PEARL MILLET (BAJRA).favorableConditions: Cool temperature (15-20°C), high humidity during flowering
PEARL MILLET (BAJRA).severity: MEDIUM to HIGH (grain loss, mycotoxin hazard)
PEARL MILLET (BAJRA).organicRemedies: Avoid late sowing, resistant varieties, remove sclerotia before sowing

PEARL MILLET (BAJRA).name: Blast (Leaf and Neck)
symptoms: Elliptical gray lesions with brown margins on leaves
PEARL MILLET (BAJRA).soilTypesAffected: All types, severe in nitrogen-rich soils
PEARL MILLET (BAJRA).organicRemedies: Trichoderma spray, resistant varieties, balanced nitrogen

PEARL MILLET (BAJRA).name: Rust
symptoms: Small circular to oval brown pustules on leaves
PEARL MILLET (BAJRA).organicRemedies: Sulfur dust, resistant varieties, field sanitation

PEARL MILLET (BAJRA).name: Smut (Long and Short)
symptoms: Panicle transformed into black dusty spore mass
Long smut: entire panicle affected, elongated
PEARL MILLET (BAJRA).organicRemedies: Resistant varieties, seed treatment with Trichoderma, rogue infected plants

CHICKPEA (BENGAL GRAM).name: Fusarium Wilt
CHICKPEA (BENGAL GRAM).teluguName: శనగ మూల వాడి పోవు వ్యాధి (Shanaga Moola Vaadi Povu Vyadhi)
symptoms: Blackening of vascular tissue (visible when stem split)
CHICKPEA (BENGAL GRAM).occurrenceInTelangana: Major problem in Black cotton soils
CHICKPEA (BENGAL GRAM).severity: VERY HIGH - Can cause total crop failure
CHICKPEA (BENGAL GRAM).organicRemedies: Resistant varieties (must use in wilt-sick areas), Trichoderma seed treatment, crop rotation with cereals

CHICKPEA (BENGAL GRAM).name: Ascochyta Blight (Black Spot)
CHICKPEA (BENGAL GRAM).teluguName: శనగ నల్ల మచ్చ వ్యాధి (Shanaga Nalla Machha Vyadhi)
symptoms: Circular gray spots with dark brown margins on leaves, stems, pods
Pod infection causes seed discoloration and quality loss
CHICKPEA (BENGAL GRAM).occurrenceInTelangana: Sporadic in Rabi season with untimely rains
CHICKPEA (BENGAL GRAM).organicRemedies: Disease-free seed, Trichoderma spray, copper fungicides

CHICKPEA (BENGAL GRAM).name: Botrytis Gray Mold
symptoms: Gray fuzzy mold on leaves, stems, flowers, pods
CHICKPEA (BENGAL GRAM).favorableConditions: Cool temperature (15-20°C), high humidity, dense canopy
CHICKPEA (BENGAL GRAM).occurrenceInTelangana: Rare, only in unusually cool humid years
CHICKPEA (BENGAL GRAM).organicRemedies: Proper plant spacing, avoid overhead irrigation, Trichoderma

CHICKPEA (BENGAL GRAM).name: Collar Rot/Root Rot
symptoms: Collar region rotting at soil level
White mycelial growth with mustard seed-like sclerotia
CHICKPEA (BENGAL GRAM).favorableConditions: High temperature, high moisture, poor drainage
CHICKPEA (BENGAL GRAM).occurrenceInTelangana: Common in heavy soils with waterlogging
CHICKPEA (BENGAL GRAM).organicRemedies: Trichoderma seed and soil treatment, proper drainage, avoid deep sowing

CHICKPEA (BENGAL GRAM).name: Dry Root Rot
CHICKPEA (BENGAL GRAM).occurrenceInTelangana: Common in late-season crop, Red soils

PIGEON PEA (RED GRAM).name: Fusarium Wilt
PIGEON PEA (RED GRAM).teluguName: కంద వాడి పోవు వ్యాధి (Kanda Vaadi Povu Vyadhi)
PIGEON PEA (RED GRAM).status: VERIFIED (Wikipedia - List of Pigeon Pea Diseases)
symptoms: Yellowing of leaves starting from lower branches
Vascular browning visible when stem split
PIGEON PEA (RED GRAM).occurrenceInTelangana: Major disease in all red gram areas
PIGEON PEA (RED GRAM).soilTypesAffected: All types, severe in wilt-sick Black soils
PIGEON PEA (RED GRAM).organicRemedies: Resistant varieties (ICP-8863, ICPL-87119), Trichoderma seed treatment, crop rotation

PIGEON PEA (RED GRAM).name: Sterility Mosaic Disease (SMD)
PIGEON PEA (RED GRAM).pathogen: Pigeon pea sterility mosaic virus (PPSMV) + Eriophyid mite vector (*Aceria cajani*)
symptoms: Small pale green or chlorotic leaves
PIGEON PEA (RED GRAM).favorableConditions: Mite population buildup, warm dry weather
PIGEON PEA (RED GRAM).occurrenceInTelangana: Major problem - endemic in many areas
PIGEON PEA (RED GRAM).organicRemedies: Resistant varieties (ICPL-87119, ICP-8863), early sowing, rogue infected plants, Neem oil spray for mite control

PIGEON PEA (RED GRAM).name: Phytophthora Blight (Stem Canker)
symptoms: Dark brown to black lesions on stem
PIGEON PEA (RED GRAM).occurrenceInTelangana: Common during heavy rains, Black soils
PIGEON PEA (RED GRAM).organicRemedies: Good drainage, ridge planting, Metalaxyl seed treatment, resistant varieties

PIGEON PEA (RED GRAM).name: Alternaria Blight
PIGEON PEA (RED GRAM).teluguName: కంద ఆకు మచ్చ వ్యాధి (Kanda Aku Machha Vyadhi)
symptoms: Circular to irregular brown spots on leaves
PIGEON PEA (RED GRAM).organicRemedies: Trichoderma spray, field sanitation, balanced nutrition

SOYBEAN.name: Rust
symptoms: Small tan to dark brown lesions on leaves
Tiny raised pustules on lower leaf surface
SOYBEAN.severity: VERY HIGH - Can cause 80% yield loss
SOYBEAN.organicRemedies: Sulfur dust, resistant varieties, timely sowing

SOYBEAN.name: Bacterial Pustule
SOYBEAN.organicRemedies: Copper spray, disease-free seed, resistant varieties

SOYBEAN.name: Anthracnose
SOYBEAN.organicRemedies: Trichoderma spray, field sanitation, resistant varieties

SOYBEAN.name: Yellow Mosaic Virus
SOYBEAN.pathogen: Mung bean yellow mosaic India virus (MYMIV) transmitted by whitefly
symptoms: Yellow and green mosaic patterns on leaves
SOYBEAN.organicRemedies: Neem oil for whitefly control, resistant varieties, early sowing, rogue infected plants

GREEN GRAM (MUNG BEAN).name: Yellow Mosaic Virus
GREEN GRAM (MUNG BEAN).pathogen: Mung bean yellow mosaic India virus (MYMIV) transmitted by whitefly (*Bemisia tabaci*)
GREEN GRAM (MUNG BEAN).status: VERIFIED (Wikipedia - List of Mung Bean Diseases)
symptoms: Bright yellow and green mosaic on leaves
GREEN GRAM (MUNG BEAN).occurrenceInTelangana: Major problem - can cause total failure
GREEN GRAM (MUNG BEAN).organicRemedies: Neem oil spray, resistant varieties, early sowing (before whitefly buildup), yellow sticky traps

GREEN GRAM (MUNG BEAN).name: Powdery Mildew
symptoms: White powdery coating on leaves, stems, pods
GREEN GRAM (MUNG BEAN).favorableConditions: Moderate temperature, dry weather with high humidity
GREEN GRAM (MUNG BEAN).organicRemedies: Sulfur dust, Neem oil, wood ash spray

GREEN GRAM (MUNG BEAN).name: Cercospora Leaf Spot
symptoms: Circular to irregular brown spots on leaves

BLACK GRAM (URD BEAN).name: Yellow Mosaic Virus
BLACK GRAM (URD BEAN).pathogen: Mung bean yellow mosaic India virus (MYMIV) transmitted by whitefly
BLACK GRAM (URD BEAN).status: VERIFIED (Wikipedia - List of Vigna mungo Diseases)
BLACK GRAM (URD BEAN).organicRemedies: Neem oil spray, resistant varieties, early sowing, rogue infected plants

BLACK GRAM (URD BEAN).name: Powdery Mildew
symptoms: White powdery growth on leaves and pods
BLACK GRAM (URD BEAN).organicRemedies: Sulfur dust, Neem oil, wood ash

BLACK GRAM (URD BEAN).name: Leaf Crinkle Virus
BLACK GRAM (URD BEAN).pathogen: Urd bean leaf crinkle virus transmitted by whitefly
BLACK GRAM (URD BEAN).organicRemedies: Neem oil spray, resistant varieties, vector control

SUGARCANE.name: Red Rot

SUGARCANE.name: Smut (Whip Smut)

Thin canes with reduced sugar content

TOBACCO.name: Tobacco Mosaic Virus (TMV)
TOBACCO.status: VERIFIED (Wikipedia - List of Tobacco Diseases, 50+ diseases documented)
symptoms: Mottled light and dark green mosaic patterns on leaves
TOBACCO.organicRemedies: Virus-free transplants, avoid handling plants when wet, rogue infected plants, wash hands/tools with milk

TOBACCO.name: Black Shank
symptoms: Black lesions on stem base and roots

TOBACCO.name: Bacterial Wilt

TOBACCO.name: Frogeye Leaf Spot

TOBACCO.name: Brown Spot

TOBACCO.name: Blue Mold

TOBACCO.occurrenceInTelangana: Sporadic in seedbeds during cool weather

TURMERIC.name: Rhizome Rot

TURMERIC.occurrenceInTelangana: Major problem in poorly drained areas

MANGO.name: Anthracnose

symptoms: Black spots on leaves, flowers, fruits
MANGO.organicRemedies: Bordeaux mixture spray, proper pruning for air circulation, post-harvest hot water treatment (52°C, 5 min)

MANGO.name: Powdery Mildew
symptoms: White powdery coating on flowers, young leaves, fruits
MANGO.favorableConditions: Dry weather with cool nights during flowering
MANGO.severity: VERY HIGH - Can cause complete flower/fruit drop
MANGO.organicRemedies: Sulfur dust/spray, Neem oil, wood ash spray, proper pruning

MANGO.name: Bacterial Canker (Bacterial Black Spot)
MANGO.organicRemedies: Copper spray (Bordeaux mixture), pruning infected branches, avoid overhead irrigation

MANGO.name: Sooty Mold
MANGO.pathogen: *Capnodium mangiferae* and other sooty mold fungi
MANGO.type: Fungal (Secondary - grows on honeydew from insects)
symptoms: Black sooty coating on leaves and fruits
MANGO.favorableConditions: Presence of sucking insects (mealybugs, scale, hoppers)
MANGO.organicRemedies: Control insects with Neem oil, wash fruits with mild soap solution

MANGO.name: Mango Malformation
symptoms: Abnormal flowers that don't set fruit
MANGO.organicRemedies: Prune out malformed panicles before flowering, Neem spray for mite control, balanced nutrition

BANANA.name: Panama Disease (Fusarium Wilt)
BANANA.teluguName: అరటి వాడి పోవు వ్యాధి (Arati Vaadi Povu Vyadhi)
BANANA.pathogen: *Fusarium oxysporum* f.sp. *cubense* (Tropical Race 4 - TR4 is devastating strain)
BANANA.status: VERIFIED (Wikipedia - List of Banana and Plantain Diseases, 50+ diseases documented)
BANANA.soilTypesAffected: All types - soil-borne, persists for decades
BANANA.severity: EXTREME - No cure, eradication of plantations
BANANA.organicRemedies: NO EFFECTIVE ORGANIC CURE. Use resistant varieties (Poovan, Robusta), avoid movement of infected planting material, quarantine measures

BANANA.name: Sigatoka Diseases (Black and Yellow)
symptoms: Streaks enlarge to spots with gray centers
BANANA.organicRemedies: Remove infected leaves, proper spacing, Bordeaux mixture, resistant varieties

BANANA.name: Bunchy Top Virus
BANANA.pathogen: Banana bunchy top virus (BBTV) transmitted by aphid (*Pentalonia nigronervosa*)
symptoms: Dark green streaks on leaf veins and midribs
Leaves short, narrow, brittle, upright (bunchy appearance)
BANANA.organicRemedies: Use virus-free suckers, rogue infected plants immediately, control aphids with Neem oil

BANANA.name: Bacterial Wilt (Moko Disease)
symptoms: Milky bacterial ooze from cut pseudostem
BANANA.organicRemedies: Remove and destroy infected plants, avoid wounding, disinfect tools, use disease-free planting material

BANANA.name: Anthracnose (Crown Rot)
symptoms: Dark brown to black spots on ripe fruits
BANANA.organicRemedies: Careful handling to avoid wounds, post-harvest fungicide dip, proper storage temperature

CITRUS.name: Citrus Canker
CITRUS.status: VERIFIED (Wikipedia - List of Citrus Diseases, 139+ diseases documented)
symptoms: Raised corky lesions on leaves, twigs, fruits
CITRUS.favorableConditions: Warm humid weather, rainfall, wind, wounds
CITRUS.organicRemedies: Copper spray (Bordeaux mixture), windbreaks, remove infected plant parts, avoid overhead irrigation

CITRUS.name: Citrus Greening (Huanglongbing - HLB)
CITRUS.pathogen: *Candidatus Liberibacter* spp. (bacterial) transmitted by Asian citrus psyllid (*Diaphorina citri*)
CITRUS.status: VERIFIED (Wikipedia - Most devastating citrus disease globally)
symptoms: Yellow shoots (blotchy mottle pattern different from nutrient deficiency)
CITRUS.severity: EXTREME - NO CURE, threatens citrus industry globally
CITRUS.organicRemedies: NO EFFECTIVE CURE. Control psyllids with Neem oil, remove infected trees, use certified disease-free nursery stock, quarantine measures

CITRUS.name: Tristeza Virus
CITRUS.pathogen: Citrus tristeza virus (CTV) transmitted by aphids
symptoms: Quick decline and death (on sour orange rootstock)
CITRUS.occurrenceInTelangana: Present but managed via rootstock selection
CITRUS.organicRemedies: Use tolerant rootstock (Rough lemon, Rangpur lime), virus-free budwood, control aphids, cross-protection with mild strains

CITRUS.name: Powdery Mildew
symptoms: White powdery growth on young leaves, shoots, flowers
CITRUS.favorableConditions: Dry weather with cool nights, shade
CITRUS.organicRemedies: Sulfur spray, Neem oil, proper sunlight exposure

CITRUS.name: Dieback (Twig Blight)
CITRUS.pathogen: *Colletotrichum gloeosporioides*, also associated with nutrient deficiency
symptoms: Drying of twigs from tips backward
CITRUS.occurrenceInTelangana: Common in old orchards, poor soils
CITRUS.soilTypesAffected: All types, severe in deficient soils
CITRUS.organicRemedies: Prune dead twigs, foliar nutrition (micronutrients - Zn, Mn, B), Trichoderma spray, balanced fertilization

CITRUS.name: Gummosis (Foot Rot)
symptoms: Gum exudation from bark at trunk base
CITRUS.organicRemedies: Improve drainage, expose root crown, scrape diseased bark and apply Bordeaux paste, avoid irrigation water contact with trunk

PAPAYA.name: Papaya Ring Spot Virus (PRSV)

PAPAYA.pathogen: Papaya ring spot virus transmitted by aphids
PAPAYA.status: VERIFIED (Wikipedia - List of Papaya Diseases, 71+ diseases documented)
symptoms: Ring spots and mosaic on fruits
Mosaic and vein clearing on leaves
PAPAYA.organicRemedies: Use resistant/tolerant varieties (transgenic available), control aphids with Neem oil, rogue infected plants, avoid new plantings near infected fields

PAPAYA.name: Powdery Mildew
symptoms: White powdery coating on leaves, stems, fruits
PAPAYA.organicRemedies: Sulfur spray, Neem oil, wood ash spray

PAPAYA.name: Anthracnose (Fruit Rot)

PAPAYA.organicRemedies: Careful harvesting, post-harvest hot water treatment, Bordeaux spray before harvest

PAPAYA.name: Phytophthora Rot (Root, Stem, Fruit)
symptoms: Stem base rotting with water-soaked lesions
PAPAYA.occurrenceInTelangana: Common in heavy rainfall, poorly drained soils
PAPAYA.organicRemedies: Improve drainage, ridge planting, avoid waterlogging, Trichoderma, Metalaxyl seed treatment

PAPAYA.name: Damping Off
PAPAYA.favorableConditions: High moisture, poor ventilation in nursery
PAPAYA.organicRemedies: Trichoderma seed treatment, avoid overwatering, proper ventilation, sterilized potting mix

WATERMELON.name: Anthracnose

WATERMELON.name: Powdery Mildew
WATERMELON.organicRemedies: Sulfur spray, Neem oil, wood ash

POMEGRANATE.name: Wilt

GRAPES.name: Powdery Mildew

GRAPES.name: Anthracnose

OKRA (BHINDI).pathogen: Not a disease - Insect pest damage
OKRA (BHINDI).organicRemedies: Neem oil spray, yellow sticky traps

CABBAGE.name: Black Rot
CABBAGE.status: VERIFIED (Wikipedia main article - Featured Article comprehensive reference)

POTATO.name: Late Blight
symptoms: Water-soaked dark brown lesions on leaves with white fungal growth on undersides
Rapid blackening and death of foliage within 7-10 days
Brown rot on tubers with granular texture
Entire crop destruction possible in humid conditions
POTATO.occurrenceInTelangana: Common in Rabi season, especially during foggy mornings
POTATO.soilTypesAffected: All soil types, especially in poorly drained areas
POTATO.severity: VERY HIGH - Can cause 100% crop loss within days
POTATO.organicRemedies: Bordeaux Mixture 1% preventive spray every 10 days, Trichoderma viride seed tuber treatment (10g/kg) + soil application (2.5kg/acre), Pseudomonas fluorescens foliar spray (10g/L)

POTATO.name: Early Blight
symptoms: Circular brown spots with concentric rings (target board pattern) on older leaves
Yellowing of leaves around spots, premature leaf drop
Reduced plant vigor and yield loss
POTATO.organicRemedies: Neem Oil 0.5-1% spray (5-10ml/L) every 7-10 days, Panchagavya 3% foliar spray (30ml/L) for immunity, Copper Oxychloride 0.3% (3g/L) as contact fungicide

POTATO.name: Bacterial Wilt
symptoms: Sudden wilting of entire plant during hot hours, initially recovers at night
Brown discoloration of vascular tissue visible when stem is cut
White bacterial ooze from cut stems when placed in water
POTATO.favorableConditions: High soil temperature (27-32°C), high moisture, acidic soils
POTATO.soilTypesAffected: All soils, worse in continuously cropped solanaceous fields
POTATO.severity: VERY HIGH - No cure once infected
POTATO.organicRemedies: Pseudomonas fluorescens seed tuber treatment (10g/kg) + soil drench (10g/L) preventively, Trichoderma harzianum soil application (2.5kg/acre) before planting, Crop rotation avoiding solanaceous crops for 3-4 years

POTATO.name: Black Scurf
symptoms: Black sclerotia (hard structures) on tuber surface resembling soil particles
Reddish-brown cankers on stems below soil level
Stunted plant growth, yellowing of lower leaves
POTATO.favorableConditions: Cool wet soils (15-18°C), heavy soils, deep planting
POTATO.soilTypesAffected: Heavy black soils, poorly drained areas
POTATO.organicRemedies: Trichoderma viride seed tuber treatment (10g/kg) + soil application, Beejamrutham seed tuber coating before planting, Jeevamrutham soil drench (200L/acre)

GINGER.name: Soft Rot/Rhizome Rot
symptoms: Water-soaked lesions on rhizomes with foul smell
Yellowing and wilting of aerial shoots (pseudo-stems)
Complete rotting of rhizomes in soil or storage
Soft, mushy texture with dark brown discoloration
GINGER.favorableConditions: Waterlogged soils, poor drainage, high rainfall, 25-30°C
GINGER.soilTypesAffected: All soils, especially waterlogged and poorly drained areas
GINGER.severity: VERY HIGH - Most destructive disease, can cause 50-80% loss
GINGER.organicRemedies: Trichoderma viride rhizome treatment (10g/kg) before planting + soil application (2.5kg/acre), Bordeaux Mixture 1% rhizome dip for 30 minutes before planting, Pseudomonas fluorescens rhizome treatment + soil drench (10g/L) monthly

GINGER.name: Bacterial Wilt
symptoms: Sudden drooping and wilting of lower leaves
Milky bacterial ooze from cut pseudo-stem base
GINGER.favorableConditions: High temperature (30-35°C), high moisture, continuous cropping
GINGER.occurrenceInTelangana: Common in Adilabad, Warangal during summer months
GINGER.soilTypesAffected: All soils, worse in continuously cropped areas
GINGER.severity: VERY HIGH - No cure, extremely contagious
GINGER.organicRemedies: Pseudomonas fluorescens preventive rhizome treatment + regular soil drench, Crop rotation 3-4 years avoiding ginger-turmeric-solanaceous crops, Bordeaux Mixture 1% soil drench in disease-prone areas

GINGER.name: Phyllosticta Leaf Spot
symptoms: Small yellow spots on leaves enlarging to brown lesions with yellow halos
Premature drying and death of leaves
Reduced photosynthesis leading to smaller rhizomes
GINGER.occurrenceInTelangana: Common during monsoon in Khammam, Warangal
GINGER.organicRemedies: Neem Oil 1% spray (10ml/L) every 10 days, Panchagavya 3% foliar spray for immunity, Bordeaux Mixture 0.5% preventive spray before monsoon

GINGER.name: Fusarium Yellows
symptoms: Yellowing starting from leaf tips and margins
Drooping and drying of leaves from bottom upward
Vascular browning in rhizomes and pseudo-stems
GINGER.favorableConditions: Poorly drained soils, high temperature (28-32°C), nematode infestation
GINGER.organicRemedies: Trichoderma harzianum rhizome treatment + soil application (more effective against Fusarium), Jeevamrutham soil drench to enhance beneficial microbes, Neem Cake 200-250 kg/acre for nematode control

GARLIC.name: Purple Blotch
symptoms: Small water-soaked lesions on leaves/stalks turning purple to brown
Concentric rings within lesions giving target-like appearance
Lesions girdle leaves causing tip die-back
Bulb quality deterioration, storage rot initiation
GARLIC.occurrenceInTelangana: Very common in Mahabubnagar, Rangareddy during Rabi season
GARLIC.organicRemedies: Bordeaux Mixture 1% preventive spray at 15-day intervals starting 30 days after planting, Neem Oil 1% (10ml/L) every 7-10 days during humid periods, Trichoderma viride foliar spray (10g/L) + soil application

GARLIC.name: Basal Rot
symptoms: Yellowing and wilting of older leaves first
Root rot with pinkish-white fungal growth on basal plate
Soft, watery rot of bulb scales from base upward
GARLIC.favorableConditions: High soil temperature (>28°C), poor ventilation in storage, injured bulbs
GARLIC.occurrenceInTelangana: Common in Medak, Nizamabad during storage
GARLIC.organicRemedies: Trichoderma harzianum clove treatment (10g/kg) + soil application before planting, Pseudomonas fluorescens clove treatment + soil drench, Proper curing - dry bulbs 2-3 weeks before storage in well-ventilated area

GARLIC.name: White Rot
symptoms: Yellowing and wilting of leaves starting from tips
Fluffy white fungal growth on bulb base and roots
Black sclerotia (mustard seed-like) in white mycelium
Complete rotting of bulbs, plant death
GARLIC.favorableConditions: Cool moist soil (10-20°C), acidic soil, continuous allium cultivation
GARLIC.occurrenceInTelangana: Less common, occurs in cooler areas
GARLIC.soilTypesAffected: All soils, worse in acidic soils
GARLIC.severity: VERY HIGH - Sclerotia survive 20+ years in soil
GARLIC.organicRemedies: Trichoderma viride + Trichoderma harzianum combined soil application (5kg/acre) with FYM 2-3 weeks before planting, Crop rotation 5-8 years avoiding allium crops, Soil solarization in summer for 6 weeks

SPINACH.name: Downy Mildew
symptoms: Pale yellow spots on upper leaf surface
Grayish-purple downy fungal growth on lower leaf surface
Leaves become chlorotic, curl, and die
Severe reduction in leaf quality and yield
SPINACH.favorableConditions: Cool humid weather (10-20°C), high humidity (>90%), dew/fog
SPINACH.occurrenceInTelangana: Common during winter in Rangareddy, Warangal
SPINACH.organicRemedies: Bordeaux Mixture 0.5% preventive spray every 10 days (lower concentration for tender leaves), Trichoderma viride seed treatment (10g/kg) + foliar spray (10g/L), Pseudomonas fluorescens foliar spray (10g/L) preventively

SPINACH.name: Cercospora Leaf Spot
symptoms: Small circular tan-brown spots with purple borders on leaves
Spots may have gray centers as disease progresses
Numerous spots cause leaves to yellow and drop
SPINACH.favorableConditions: Warm humid weather (24-28°C), overhead irrigation, dense planting
SPINACH.organicRemedies: Neem Oil 0.5% (5ml/L) spray every 7 days, Panchagavya 2-3% immunity booster every 10-15 days, Proper spacing for air circulation, avoid overhead irrigation

SPINACH.name: Damping Off
symptoms: Seeds decay before germination (pre-emergence damping off)
Seedlings collapse at soil level with water-soaked lesions
Brown discoloration at stem base, white fungal threads visible
Patches of dead seedlings in nursery/field
SPINACH.favorableConditions: Excessive moisture, poor drainage, heavy soils, deep sowing
SPINACH.organicRemedies: Trichoderma viride seed treatment (10g/kg) + seedbed soil treatment, Beejamrutham seed coating before sowing, Raised beds with improved drainage, avoid over-watering

BROCCOLI.name: Downy Mildew
symptoms: Yellow angular spots on upper leaf surface confined by veins
White-gray downy fungal growth on lower leaf surface
Leaves become chlorotic, curl, and die prematurely
BROCCOLI.favorableConditions: Cool humid conditions (15-20°C), high humidity (>85%), cloudy weather
BROCCOLI.occurrenceInTelangana: Common during winter in Rangareddy, Medak
BROCCOLI.organicRemedies: Bordeaux Mixture 0.5-1% preventive spray every 10-12 days, Trichoderma viride seed treatment + foliar spray (10g/L), Pseudomonas fluorescens foliar spray (10g/L)

BROCCOLI.name: Black Rot
symptoms: V-shaped yellow lesions on leaf margins progressing inward
Vascular discoloration in stems and heads, foul smell
BROCCOLI.favorableConditions: Warm humid weather (25-30°C), insect damage, water splash
BROCCOLI.organicRemedies: Pseudomonas fluorescens seed treatment + seedling dip + foliar spray (10g/L), Copper Oxychloride 0.25-0.3% spray every 10 days, Bordeaux Mixture 1%, remove infected plants immediately

BROCCOLI.name: Clubroot
symptoms: Stunted plant growth, yellowing of leaves
Wilting during hot hours, poor head development
Plants may die prematurely, severe yield loss
BROCCOLI.favorableConditions: Acidic soils (pH <7.0), waterlogged conditions, continuous brassica cropping
BROCCOLI.occurrenceInTelangana: Rare but can occur in acidic soils
BROCCOLI.organicRemedies: Lime application to raise soil pH to 7.0-7.2, Trichoderma viride heavy soil application (5kg/acre) with FYM, Crop rotation 4-5 years avoiding all brassica crops, improve drainage

BROCCOLI.name: Alternaria Leaf Spot
symptoms: Dark brown circular spots with concentric rings
Yellow halos around spots, spots coalesce causing blight
Head quality deterioration, dark spots on florets
BROCCOLI.favorableConditions: Warm humid weather (20-28°C), rain splash, dense canopy
BROCCOLI.organicRemedies: Neem Oil 1% (10ml/L) spray every 7-10 days, Panchagavya 3% for plant immunity, Bordeaux Mixture 0.5% at 15-day intervals

IVY GOURD.name: Powdery Mildew
symptoms: White powdery fungal growth on upper and lower leaf surfaces
Yellowing and curling of affected leaves
Premature leaf drop, reduced fruit quality
Stunted vine growth and reduced yield
IVY GOURD.favorableConditions: Moderate temperature (20-28°C), low to moderate humidity, dry weather
IVY GOURD.occurrenceInTelangana: Common during cool dry season in Warangal, Karimnagar
IVY GOURD.organicRemedies: Neem Oil 1% + Buttermilk spray (10:1 ratio), Pseudomonas fluorescens foliar spray (10g/L), Panchagavya 3% weekly application

IVY GOURD.name: Downy Mildew
symptoms: Angular yellow spots on upper leaf surface limited by veins
Grayish-purple downy growth on lower leaf surface
Leaves become necrotic and drop prematurely
IVY GOURD.favorableConditions: High humidity (>85%), cool temperatures (15-22°C), dew formation
IVY GOURD.organicRemedies: Bordeaux Mixture 1% spray every 7-10 days, Trichoderma viride foliar spray (10g/L), Proper spacing for air circulation

IVY GOURD.name: Anthracnose
symptoms: Circular sunken lesions on fruits with pinkish spore masses
Dark brown to black spots on leaves and stems
Fruit rot starting from flower end
Premature fruit drop and reduced market quality
IVY GOURD.favorableConditions: Warm humid weather (25-30°C), rain splash, wounded fruits
IVY GOURD.organicRemedies: Neem Oil 1% + Garlic extract spray, Bordeaux Mixture 0.5%, Trichoderma harzianum foliar application

BROAD BEANS.name: Rust
symptoms: Reddish-brown pustules on leaves and pods
Severe leaf yellowing and premature defoliation
Reduced pod filling and seed quality
Plant weakening and yield loss up to 50%
BROAD BEANS.favorableConditions: Cool humid conditions (15-22°C), dew formation, dense canopy
BROAD BEANS.occurrenceInTelangana: Common during winter in Rangareddy, Medak
BROAD BEANS.organicRemedies: Bordeaux Mixture 1% spray every 10 days, Neem Oil 1% alternating with Panchagavya 3%, Sulfur dust application

BROAD BEANS.name: Chocolate Spot
symptoms: Chocolate brown spots on leaves, stems, and pods
Spots become sunken with reddish-brown margins
BROAD BEANS.favorableConditions: Cool humid weather (18-25°C), high humidity (>80%), nitrogen deficiency
BROAD BEANS.soilTypesAffected: All soil types, worse in nitrogen-deficient soils
BROAD BEANS.organicRemedies: Bordeaux Mixture 1% preventive spray, Trichoderma viride foliar (10g/L), Balanced nutrition with organic manures

BROAD BEANS.name: Powdery Mildew
symptoms: White powdery growth on leaves, stems, and pods
BROAD BEANS.favorableConditions: Moderate temperature (22-28°C), low humidity, dry weather
BROAD BEANS.organicRemedies: Buttermilk spray (1:10 with water), Neem Oil 1%, Pseudomonas fluorescens foliar spray

CLUSTER BEANS.name: Bacterial Leaf Spot
symptoms: Small water-soaked lesions on leaves becoming necrotic
CLUSTER BEANS.favorableConditions: Warm humid weather (25-32°C), rain splash, overhead irrigation
CLUSTER BEANS.occurrenceInTelangana: Common during Kharif in Mahabubnagar, Nagarkurnool
CLUSTER BEANS.organicRemedies: Pseudomonas fluorescens spray (10g/L) every 7-10 days, Copper Oxychloride 0.3%, Bordeaux Mixture 1% preventive

CLUSTER BEANS.name: Alternaria Leaf Spot
symptoms: Brown circular spots with concentric rings on leaves
CLUSTER BEANS.organicRemedies: Neem Oil 1% spray weekly, Trichoderma viride foliar application, Bordeaux Mixture 0.5%

CLUSTER BEANS.name: Powdery Mildew
symptoms: White powdery growth primarily on lower leaf surface
Yellow chlorotic patches on upper surface
CLUSTER BEANS.organicRemedies: Buttermilk + Neem Oil spray, Pseudomonas fluorescens (10g/L), Panchagavya 3%

MINT.name: Mint Rust
symptoms: Orange-brown pustules on lower leaf surface
Yellow spots on upper leaf surface
MINT.favorableConditions: Cool humid conditions (15-22°C), high humidity, dew formation
MINT.organicRemedies: Bordeaux Mixture 1% spray every 7 days, Remove infected plants immediately, Sulfur dust application, Plant disease-free stolons

MINT.name: Verticillium Wilt
symptoms: Yellowing of lower leaves progressing upward
Stunted growth, plant death in severe cases
MINT.occurrenceInTelangana: Can occur in continuously cropped areas
MINT.organicRemedies: Trichoderma harzianum soil application (5kg/acre), Crop rotation with non-host crops, Remove and destroy infected plants, Use disease-free planting material

MINT.name: Anthracnose
symptoms: Dark brown sunken lesions on leaves and stems
MINT.organicRemedies: Bordeaux Mixture 0.5%, Neem Oil + Garlic extract, Trichoderma viride foliar spray

GONGURA (RED SORREL).name: Cercospora/Alternaria Leaf Spot
symptoms: Circular brown spots with yellow halos on leaves
Severe spotting reduces leaf quality for harvest
Premature leaf drop in severe cases
GONGURA (RED SORREL).favorableConditions: Warm humid weather (24-30°C), rain splash, dense planting
GONGURA (RED SORREL).occurrenceInTelangana: Very common during Kharif across all districts
GONGURA (RED SORREL).organicRemedies: Bordeaux Mixture 0.5-1% (use lower concentration for tender leaves), Neem Oil 0.5-1%, Panchagavya 3% for plant immunity

GONGURA (RED SORREL).name: Powdery Mildew
symptoms: White powdery growth on leaves (both surfaces)
GONGURA (RED SORREL).favorableConditions: Moderate temperature (22-28°C), low to moderate humidity
GONGURA (RED SORREL).occurrenceInTelangana: Common during dry spells in growing season
GONGURA (RED SORREL).organicRemedies: Buttermilk spray (1:10 dilution), Neem Oil 1%, Pseudomonas fluorescens foliar spray

GONGURA (RED SORREL).name: Root Rot
symptoms: Wilting of plants despite adequate moisture
Patches of dead plants in field
GONGURA (RED SORREL).favorableConditions: Waterlogged soils, poor drainage, heavy rains
GONGURA (RED SORREL).occurrenceInTelangana: Common in poorly drained fields during monsoon
GONGURA (RED SORREL).organicRemedies: Trichoderma viride soil application + seed treatment, Improve drainage with raised beds, Avoid over-watering, Jeevamrutham soil drench

GONGURA (RED SORREL).name: Yellow Vein Mosaic Virus
symptoms: Yellowing of veins creating mosaic pattern
GONGURA (RED SORREL).organicRemedies: Neem Oil 1% for whitefly control, Yellow sticky traps, Remove infected plants, Neem Cake soil application to repel whiteflies, Reflective mulches

organicRemedies.name: Trichoderma viride / T. harzianum
organicRemedies.status: VERIFIED - Extensively tested by PJTSAU, ICAR institutes
organicRemedies.target: Wilt, root rot, seed rot, damping off, sheath blight
organicRemedies.application: Seed treatment (10g/kg seed), Soil application (2.5 kg/acre mixed with FYM), Foliar spray (5g/liter)
organicRemedies.preparation: Can be purchased as formulated product or prepared on-farm using rice bran/wheat bran substrate

organicRemedies.name: Pseudomonas fluorescens
organicRemedies.target: Bacterial blight, bacterial wilt, damping off, blast
organicRemedies.modeOfAction: Antibiotic production, siderophore competition, induced systemic resistance
organicRemedies.application: Seed treatment (10g/kg), Seedling root dip (10g/liter for 30 min), Soil application (2.5 kg/acre)

organicRemedies.name: Neem Oil (Azadirachtin-based)
organicRemedies.target: Powdery mildew, leaf curl virus (vector control), anthracnose
organicRemedies.modeOfAction: Antifeedant, repellent, growth regulator (insects), direct fungicidal effect
organicRemedies.application: Foliar spray (3-5 ml/liter water), 2-3 sprays at 10-day interval
organicRemedies.preparation: Cold-pressed neem oil (organic shops) or neem kernel extract

organicRemedies.name: Panchagavya
organicRemedies.target: General plant immunity booster, powdery mildew, leaf spots
organicRemedies.application: Foliar spray (3% solution - 30 ml/liter), Soil drench
organicRemedies.ingredients: Cow dung, cow urine, milk, curd, ghee, jaggery, banana, coconut water (fermented 15-20 days)

organicRemedies.name: Jeevamrutham
organicRemedies.status: VERIFIED - Natural farming, PJTSAU organic recommendations
organicRemedies.ingredients: Cow dung (10 kg), cow urine (10 liters), jaggery (2 kg), pulse flour (2 kg), soil (handful), water (200 liters) - fermented 5-7 days
organicRemedies.effectiveness: MEDIUM (Indirect - improves soil health and plant vigor)

organicRemedies.name: Cow Urine (Gomutra)
organicRemedies.status: VERIFIED - Traditional farming, tested for fungicidal properties
organicRemedies.application: Diluted spray (1:10 with water), mix with Neem oil for enhanced effect

organicRemedies.name: Garlic Bulb Extract
organicRemedies.preparation: 100g garlic cloves crushed + soaked in 50 ml kerosene overnight, then diluted to 1 liter with water, filter and spray

organicRemedies.name: Wood Ash
organicRemedies.status: VERIFIED - Traditional method, validated for powdery mildew
organicRemedies.application: Dust on affected plants early morning (when dew present), or spray (10% solution in water)

organicRemedies.name: Chilli + Garlic + Soap Spray
organicRemedies.teluguName: మిరపకాయ వెల్లుల్లి సబ్బు మిశ్రమం (Mirapakaya Vellulli Sabbu Mishramam)
organicRemedies.status: NON-VERIFIED (Widely used by farmers, limited scientific testing)
organicRemedies.preparation: 100g green chilli + 100g garlic + 100g soap flakes, grind and mix in 10 liters water

metadata.title: ADDITIONAL VEGETABLES - DISEASE & REMEDY DATABASE
metadata.subtitle: Telangana State - Organic Farming Focus
metadata.description: 5 Important Vegetables with Complete Disease Information

vegetables.name: Ivy Gourd
vegetables.importance: Popular backyard vegetable, high demand in local markets, medicinal properties (anti-diabetic), tender shoots and fruits consumed

diseases.name: Powdery Mildew
symptoms: White powdery coating on both leaf surfaces (upper surface mainly)
Yellowing and curling of affected leaves
Reduced vine vigor and fruit quality/yield
diseases.favorableConditions: Dry weather with moderate temperatures (20-28°C), low to moderate humidity, poor air circulation
diseases.severity: High - Can cause 30-50% yield loss

diseases.name: Downy Mildew
symptoms: Yellow angular spots on upper leaf surface confined by veins
Gray-purple downy fungal growth on lower leaf surface
Spots turn brown and necrotic, leaves become brittle
Severe defoliation in 7-10 days if untreated
diseases.favorableConditions: High humidity (>85%), cool nights with heavy dew, rain splash, temperature 15-22°C
diseases.severity: Very High - Can destroy entire crop within 2 weeks

diseases.name: Anthracnose / Fruit Rot
symptoms: Circular water-soaked lesions on fruits with sunken centers
Pink to orange spore masses in concentric rings during moist conditions
Leaf spots with brown margins and gray centers
diseases.favorableConditions: High humidity, warm weather (25-30°C), prolonged leaf wetness, fruit injuries
diseases.severity: High - 20-40% fruit loss, poor marketability

organicRemedies.dosage: 100g each crushed, boiled in 1L water, diluted 1:10

diseases.name: Root Rot / Wilt Complex
diseases.pathogen: Fusarium solani, Rhizoctonia solani, Pythium spp.
symptoms: Yellowing of vines starting from lower leaves
Wilting during hot hours, initially recovering at night, then permanent
Dark brown discoloration of roots and collar region
Plant death within 10-15 days, entire vine collapses
diseases.favorableConditions: Waterlogged soils, poor drainage, continuous cultivation in same location, high soil temperature
diseases.severity: High - Individual vine loss but can spread through field
diseases.soilTypesAffected: Black Cotton Soils (waterlogging), poorly drained areas

organicRemedies.dosage: 10g/L around root zone, repeat after 15 days
Root zone application 10g/plant mixed with FYM

vegetables.name: Broad Beans
vegetables.importance: Protein-rich vegetable, green manure crop, nitrogen fixation (enriches soil), popular in urban markets

diseases.name: Rust
symptoms: Small reddish-brown pustules (uredinia) on leaves, stems, and pods
Pustules rupture releasing rust-colored spore powder
Yellowing of surrounding tissue, premature leaf fall
Severe infection causes complete defoliation and pod shriveling
diseases.favorableConditions: High humidity (>80%), moderate temperatures (15-25°C), dense planting, prolonged dew
diseases.severity: Very High - Can cause 30-70% yield loss
diseases.soilTypesAffected: All (especially in Rabi with high dew)

organicRemedies.dosage: Spray at first sign, repeat every 10 days

diseases.name: Chocolate Spot / Leaf & Pod Spot
symptoms: Small chocolate-brown spots on leaves, enlarging to cause blighting
Similar lesions on stems, flowers, and pods
Infected pods develop sunken black lesions, seeds get discolored
In severe cases, entire plant turns black and dies (aggressive phase)
diseases.favorableConditions: Cool moist weather (10-20°C), high humidity, nitrogen deficiency, dense canopy
diseases.severity: Very High - Most destructive disease of broad beans

diseases.name: Powdery Mildew
symptoms: White powdery patches on upper and lower leaf surfaces
Affected leaves curl, turn yellow, and dry up
Reduced pod setting and pod filling
diseases.favorableConditions: Dry weather with moderate temperature (20-25°C), low humidity, poor air circulation
diseases.severity: Medium-High - 15-30% yield reduction
diseases.soilTypesAffected: Black Cotton Soils, Red Sandy Loams (dry periods)

organicRemedies.dosage: 1kg neem cake soaked in 10L water overnight, filtered, spray

diseases.name: Bean Leaf Roll Virus / Yellow Mosaic
diseases.pathogen: Bean leaf roll virus (BLRV), Bean yellow mosaic virus (BYMV)
symptoms: Yellowing and mosaic patterns on young leaves
Downward rolling and thickening of leaves
Reduced flowering and pod formation, small pods
diseases.favorableConditions: High aphid population, warm dry weather (aphid multiplication), proximity to alternate hosts
diseases.severity: High - No cure, prevention is key

organicRemedies.dosage: 5ml/L spray to control aphids every 5-7 days
50g neem seed powder/L, overnight soak, spray

organicRemedies.remedy: Remove infected plants + yellow sticky traps + reflective mulch

vegetables.name: Cluster Beans
vegetables.cropType: Annual legume vegetable (also grown for gum)
vegetables.importance: Vegetable (tender pods), guar gum (industrial use), green manure, drought-tolerant, improves soil health

diseases.name: Bacterial Leaf Spot / Blight
symptoms: Small water-soaked angular spots on leaves turning brown with yellow halo
Spots coalesce causing large blighted areas
Pod lesions as dark sunken spots reducing marketability
diseases.favorableConditions: High humidity, warm temperature (25-30°C), rain splash, overhead irrigation
diseases.severity: High - 20-40% yield loss
diseases.soilTypesAffected: All (severe during monsoon with heavy rains)

diseases.name: Alternaria Leaf Spot
symptoms: Circular to irregular brown spots with concentric rings (target-board pattern)
Spots enlarge and coalesce, causing blighting of leaves
Premature defoliation starting from lower leaves
diseases.favorableConditions: Warm humid weather (24-28°C), moderate rainfall, nutrient deficiency
diseases.severity: Medium-High - 15-30% yield reduction

diseases.name: Powdery Mildew
symptoms: White powdery growth on lower leaf surface initially, then upper surface
Yellowing and drying of affected leaves
diseases.favorableConditions: Dry weather with cool nights, low humidity, temperature 20-28°C
diseases.severity: Medium - 10-25% yield loss
diseases.soilTypesAffected: Red Sandy Loams, Black soils (during dry spells)

diseases.name: Root Rot / Wilt
symptoms: Yellowing and wilting of leaves during hot afternoon hours
Browning and rotting of tap root and lateral roots
Vascular browning visible when stem is split
diseases.favorableConditions: High soil temperature (>30°C), moisture stress followed by irrigation, poor drainage
diseases.severity: High - Can cause patchy to widespread crop loss
diseases.soilTypesAffected: Red Sandy Loams (moisture stress), poorly drained Black soils

organicRemedies.dosage: 2.5kg/acre mixed with FYM before sowing + seedling dip
Seed treatment 10g/kg + soil drench 10g/L at 30 DAS

vegetables.name: Mint
suitableSoilTypes: Red sandy loams with high organic matter
vegetables.season: Year-round (perennial), peak growth during Kharif & post-monsoon
vegetables.importance: Culinary herb (chutneys, beverages), essential oil extraction, medicinal (digestive, respiratory), high economic value

diseases.name: Mint Rust
symptoms: Small yellow spots on upper leaf surface developing into orange-brown pustules below
Pustules rupture releasing orange rust-colored spores
diseases.favorableConditions: High humidity (>80%), moderate temperature (18-24°C), dense planting, poor air circulation
diseases.severity: Very High - Most serious disease of mint, can cause total crop loss

diseases.name: Verticillium Wilt
symptoms: Yellowing of lower leaves progressing upward
Vascular browning visible in stem cross-section
Stunting and death of entire plant
diseases.favorableConditions: Cool to moderate soil temperature (20-25°C), continuous mint cultivation, infected planting material
diseases.severity: High - Can persist in soil for years
diseases.soilTypesAffected: All (especially in continuous mint cultivation areas)

organicRemedies.dosage: Soil application 2.5kg/acre + runner/stolon treatment before planting
Rotate with non-host crops for 2 years

diseases.name: Anthracnose / Leaf Blight
symptoms: Small brown to black angular spots on leaves
diseases.favorableConditions: High humidity, warm temperature (25-30°C), overhead irrigation, wounds
diseases.severity: Medium-High - 20-35% foliage loss

diseases.name: Powdery Mildew
symptoms: White powdery coating on leaves and tender stems
Reduced plant vigor and aromatic oil quality
diseases.favorableConditions: Moderate temperature (20-28°C), low to moderate humidity, shaded conditions
diseases.severity: Medium - 15-25% yield and quality reduction

vegetables.name: Red Sorrel / Gongura
majorDistricts: All districts - Most popular in Khammam
vegetables.importance: Cultural significance (Telangana state's signature ingredient), rich in Vitamin C & iron, used in pickles, curries, chutneys, antioxidant properties, very high market demand

diseases.name: Leaf Spot / Cercospora Leaf Spot
symptoms: Small circular to angular brown spots with gray centers on leaves
Spots coalesce causing large blighted areas
diseases.favorableConditions: High humidity (>80%), warm temperature (25-30°C), rain splash, dense planting
diseases.severity: High - 30-50% leaf yield loss, reduced marketability

diseases.name: Alternaria Leaf Blight
symptoms: Large irregular brown to black spots with concentric rings (target-board)
Rapid expansion of lesions during humid weather
Premature leaf drop and stem lesions
diseases.favorableConditions: Warm humid weather (24-28°C), moderate to heavy rainfall, nutrient stress
diseases.severity: High - Major disease in Telangana during Kharif

diseases.name: Powdery Mildew
symptoms: White powdery growth on both leaf surfaces
Reduced leaf size and quality (bitter taste)
diseases.favorableConditions: Dry weather with cool nights, moderate temperature (18-26°C), low humidity
diseases.severity: Medium - 15-30% quality and yield reduction
diseases.soilTypesAffected: Red Sandy Loams, Black soils (dry periods post-monsoon)

diseases.name: Root Rot / Collar Rot
diseases.pathogen: Rhizoctonia solani, Pythium spp., Phytophthora spp.
symptoms: Water-soaked lesions at collar/stem base turning dark brown
Wilting of entire plant despite adequate soil moisture
Rotting of roots with foul odor
diseases.favorableConditions: Waterlogged conditions, poor drainage, high rainfall, heavy soils
diseases.severity: High - Can cause significant crop loss in poorly drained fields
diseases.soilTypesAffected: Black Cotton Soils (waterlogging), heavy clay soils, low-lying areas

organicRemedies.dosage: Seed treatment 10g/kg + soil drench 10g/L at 20 & 40 DAS

organicRemedies.remedy: Improve field drainage + raised beds + Neem cake 200kg/acre

diseases.name: Yellow Vein Mosaic Virus
diseases.pathogen: Hibiscus yellow vein mosaic virus (HYVMV) - Begomovirus
symptoms: Yellowing of veins creating a network pattern on leaves
Reduced leaf yield and poor quality (tough texture)
diseases.favorableConditions: High whitefly population, hot dry weather (whitefly multiplication), continuous cropping
diseases.severity: High - No cure available, prevention is critical

organicRemedies.dosage: 5ml/L spray every 5-7 days for whitefly control

organicRemedies.remedy: Early removal of infected plants + reflective mulch + border crops (maize/sorghum)

culturalPractices: Crop Rotation: Rotate with non-related crops (cereals, millets) for 2-3 seasons
Field Sanitation: Remove and destroy infected plant parts, avoid crop residue
Proper Spacing: Ensure adequate plant spacing for air circulation
Drainage: Maintain good field drainage, avoid waterlogging
Resistant Varieties: Select locally adapted varieties with disease tolerance
Certified Seeds/Planting Material: Use disease-free seeds/runners/stolons
Irrigation Management: Avoid overhead irrigation, prefer drip irrigation
Balanced Nutrition: Apply organic manures (FYM, vermicompost), avoid excess nitrogen
soilHealthManagement: Trichoderma application: 2.5 kg/acre mixed with 100kg FYM before sowing
Neem cake: 200 kg/acre as soil amendment (nematode control + fertilizer)
Green manuring: Grow and incorporate dhaincha/sunhemp before vegetable cultivation
sprayScheduleKharif: 35 DAS: Bordeaux mixture 1% (if weather is humid/rainy)
55 DAS onward: Repeat based on weather and disease incidence (every 10-15 days)
vectorControl: Reflective mulch: Silver/white plastic mulch to repel insects
Border crops: Plant tall crops like maize, sorghum as barrier
Rogue infected plants: Remove immediately to prevent spread

items: Neem cake - 100kg (₹2000-3000, also serves as fertilizer)
Cultural practices - field sanitation, spacing, drainage (Labor cost)
Neem oil for foliar spray (2-3 liters)

homemadePreparations: Panchagavya: Self-prepare using cow products (₹200 for 10L)
Buttermilk spray: Use leftover buttermilk (Minimal cost)
Neem leaf extract: Collect neem leaves, crush, soak (Free)
Bordeaux mixture: Buy raw materials and prepare (₹500 vs ₹1500 ready-made)
